1→-- MMS Database Functions & Triggers Migration
     2→-- Business logic implemented at database level for consistency
     3→--
     4→-- Run order: 3 (after schema creation)
     5→-- @see specs/003-database-schema.md Section 3.6
     6→
     7→-- =============================================================================
     8→-- HELPER FUNCTIONS
     9→-- =============================================================================
    10→
    11→-- updated_at trigger function (reusable)
    12→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
    13→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    14→BEGIN
    15→    NEW.updated_at = NOW();
    16→    RETURN NEW;
    17→END;
    18→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
    19→
    20→-- =============================================================================
    21→-- TRAIN FITNESS SYNC FROM UNAVAILABILITY EVENTS
    22→-- =============================================================================
    23→
    24→-- Automatically update train.fitness_status when unavailability events change
    25→-- Priority: GROUNDED > RESTRICTED > FIT
    26→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()
    27→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    28→DECLARE
    29→    has_grounded BOOLEAN;
    30→    has_restricted BOOLEAN;
    31→    new_status fitness_status;
    32→BEGIN
    33→    -- Determine train ID to update
    34→    -- Handle both INSERT/UPDATE (NEW) and DELETE (OLD)
    35→    DECLARE
    36→        target_train_id UUID := COALESCE(NEW.train_id, OLD.train_id);
    37→    BEGIN
    38→        -- Check for active GROUNDED events
    39→        SELECT EXISTS (
    40→            SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events
    41→            WHERE train_id = target_train_id
    42→            AND status = 'ACTIVE'
    43→            AND restriction_level = 'GROUNDED'
    44→        ) INTO has_grounded;
    45→
    46→        -- Check for active RESTRICTED events
    47→        SELECT EXISTS (
    48→            SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events
    49→            WHERE train_id = target_train_id
    50→            AND status = 'ACTIVE'
    51→            AND restriction_level = 'RESTRICTED'
    52→        ) INTO has_restricted;
    53→
    54→        -- Determine new fitness status (priority order)
    55→        IF has_grounded THEN
    56→            new_status := 'GROUNDED';
    57→        ELSIF has_restricted THEN
    58→            new_status := 'RESTRICTED';
    59→        ELSE
    60→            new_status := 'FIT';
    61→        END IF;
    62→
    63→        -- Update train only if status changed
    64→        UPDATE trains
    65→        SET fitness_status = new_status,
    66→            updated_at = NOW()
    67→        WHERE id = target_train_id
    68→        AND fitness_status IS DISTINCT FROM new_status;
    69→
    70→        RETURN COALESCE(NEW, OLD);
    71→    END;
    72→END;
    73→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
    74→
    75→-- Trigger for unavailability_events changes
    76→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_sync_train_fitness ON unavailability_events;
    77→CREATE TRIGGER trg_sync_train_fitness
    78→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON unavailability_events
    79→    FOR EACH ROW
    80→    EXECUTE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability();
    81→
    82→-- =============================================================================
    83→-- MATERIALIZED VIEW REFRESH (CONCURRENT)
    84→-- =============================================================================
    85→
    86→-- Refresh all canvas-related materialized views concurrently
    87→-- Called by scheduled job or on-demand via API
    88→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
    89→RETURNS VOID AS $$
    90→BEGIN
    91→    -- Refresh strategic canvas view (yearly aggregates)
    92→    IF EXISTS (
    93→        SELECT 1 FROM pg_matviews WHERE matviewname = 'v_canvas_strategic'
    94→    ) THEN
    95→        REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
    96→    END IF;
    97→
    98→    -- Refresh tactical canvas view (weekly details)
    99→    IF EXISTS (
   100→        SELECT 1 FROM pg_matviews WHERE matviewname = 'v_canvas_tactical'
   101→    ) THEN
   102→        REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
   103→    END IF;
   104→
   105→    -- Note: v_manhour_canvas and v_drift_canvas are regular views (not materialized)
   106→    -- They don't need refresh as they query live data
   107→
   108→    RAISE NOTICE 'Canvas views refreshed at %', NOW();
   109→END;
   110→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   111→
   112→-- =============================================================================
   113→-- SCENARIO EXPIRATION CLEANUP
   114→-- =============================================================================
   115→
   116→-- Clean up expired scenarios (30-day inactivity default)
   117→-- Called by scheduled job (e.g., daily at 2 AM)
   118→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
   119→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
   120→DECLARE
   121→    deleted_count INTEGER;
   122→BEGIN
   123→    -- Mark expired scenarios as EXPIRED
   124→    WITH expired AS (
   125→        UPDATE scenarios
   126→        SET status = 'EXPIRED',
   127→            updated_at = NOW()
   128→        WHERE status = 'ACTIVE'
   129→        AND expires_at < NOW()
   130→        RETURNING id
   131→    )
   132→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO deleted_count FROM expired;
   133→
   134→    -- Delete scenario deltas for expired scenarios older than 90 days
   135→    -- (keep for audit trail for 90 days after expiration)
   136→    DELETE FROM scenario_deltas
   137→    WHERE scenario_id IN (
   138→        SELECT id FROM scenarios
   139→        WHERE status = 'EXPIRED'
   140→        AND expires_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
   141→    );
   142→
   143→    -- Log cleanup action
   144→    IF deleted_count > 0 THEN
   145→        INSERT INTO audit_log (
   146→            entity_type, entity_id, action, details, created_at
   147→        ) VALUES (
   148→            'scenario',
   149→            '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'::UUID,
   150→            'BULK_EXPIRE',
   151→            jsonb_build_object('expired_count', deleted_count),
   152→            NOW()
   153→        );
   154→    END IF;
   155→
   156→    RETURN deleted_count;
   157→END;
   158→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   159→
   160→-- =============================================================================
   161→-- MATERIAL RESERVATION CLEANUP
   162→-- =============================================================================
   163→
   164→-- Release soft reservations that have expired
   165→-- Soft reservations: quantity_reserved at 90 days, convert to hard at 14 days
   166→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
   167→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
   168→DECLARE
   169→    released_count INTEGER;
   170→BEGIN
   171→    -- Release SOFT reservations older than 90 days
   172→    WITH released AS (
   173→        UPDATE material_reservations
   174→        SET status = 'EXPIRED',
   175→            updated_at = NOW()
   176→        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
   177→        AND status = 'RESERVED'
   178→        AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
   179→        RETURNING id, inventory_position_id, quantity_reserved
   180→    ),
   181→    -- Update inventory positions to release reserved quantity
   182→    inventory_updates AS (
   183→        UPDATE inventory_positions ip
   184→        SET quantity_reserved = ip.quantity_reserved - r.quantity_reserved,
   185→            quantity_available = ip.quantity_available + r.quantity_reserved,
   186→            updated_at = NOW()
   187→        FROM released r
   188→        WHERE ip.id = r.inventory_position_id
   189→    )
   190→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO released_count FROM released;
   191→
   192→    IF released_count > 0 THEN
   193→        RAISE NOTICE 'Released % expired soft reservations', released_count;
   194→    END IF;
   195→
   196→    RETURN released_count;
   197→END;
   198→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   199→
   200→-- =============================================================================
   201→-- FLEET MILEAGE TARGET CALCULATION
   202→-- =============================================================================
   203→
   204→-- Recalculate target_mileage_km for all trains in a fleet
   205→-- Used for fleet balancing: equalizes mileage across trains
   206→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets(p_fleet_id UUID)
   207→RETURNS VOID AS $$
   208→DECLARE
   209→    fleet_avg_mileage NUMERIC;
   210→    fleet_total_target NUMERIC;
   211→    train_count INTEGER;
   212→    target_per_train NUMERIC;
   213→BEGIN
   214→    -- Get fleet statistics
   215→    SELECT
   216→        AVG(current_mileage_km),
   217→        SUM(current_mileage_km),
   218→        COUNT(*)
   219→    INTO fleet_avg_mileage, fleet_total_target, train_count
   220→    FROM trains
   221→    WHERE fleet_id = p_fleet_id
   222→    AND fitness_status != 'GROUNDED';
   223→
   224→    IF train_count = 0 THEN
   225→        RAISE NOTICE 'No active trains in fleet %', p_fleet_id;
   226→        RETURN;
   227→    END IF;
   228→
   229→    -- Calculate target: aim for equal distribution
   230→    -- Target = fleet average (equalizes wear across fleet)
   231→    target_per_train := ROUND(fleet_avg_mileage);
   232→
   233→    -- Update all trains in fleet
   234→    UPDATE trains
   235→    SET target_mileage_km = target_per_train,
   236→        updated_at = NOW()
   237→    WHERE fleet_id = p_fleet_id
   238→    AND fitness_status != 'GROUNDED';
   239→
   240→    RAISE NOTICE 'Updated % trains in fleet % with target mileage %',
   241→        train_count, p_fleet_id, target_per_train;
   242→END;
   243→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   244→
   245→-- =============================================================================
   246→-- PM HIERARCHY SATISFACTION
   247→-- =============================================================================
   248→
   249→-- When a higher PM (e.g., C4) is completed, satisfy lower PMs (C1, C2, C3)
   250→-- This resets the lower PM counters as per PM hierarchy rules
   251→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION apply_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction()
   252→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   253→DECLARE
   254→    satisfied_codes TEXT[];
   255→    pm_code TEXT;
   256→BEGIN
   257→    -- Only trigger on PM completion
   258→    IF NEW.status != 'COMPLETED' OR OLD.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN
   259→        RETURN NEW;
   260→    END IF;
   261→
   262→    -- Get satisfies_codes for this PM type
   263→    SELECT pt.satisfies_codes, pt.pm_code
   264→    INTO satisfied_codes, pm_code
   265→    FROM pm_types pt
   266→    WHERE pt.id = NEW.pm_type_id;
   267→
   268→    IF satisfied_codes IS NULL OR array_length(satisfied_codes, 1) IS NULL THEN
   269→        RETURN NEW;
   270→    END IF;
   271→
   272→    -- Update pm_tracking for all satisfied PM types
   273→    UPDATE pm_tracking
   274→    SET last_pm_date = NEW.actual_completion_date,
   275→        last_pm_mileage_km = NEW.actual_mileage_km,
   276→        updated_at = NOW()
   277→    WHERE train_id = NEW.train_id
   278→    AND pm_type_id IN (
   279→        SELECT id FROM pm_types
   280→        WHERE pm_code = ANY(satisfied_codes)
   281→        AND fleet_id = (
   282→            SELECT fleet_id FROM trains WHERE id = NEW.train_id
   283→        )
   284→    );
   285→
   286→    -- Log the cascade
   287→    INSERT INTO audit_log (
   288→        entity_type, entity_id, action, user_id, details, created_at
   289→    ) VALUES (
   290→        'pm_schedule', NEW.id, 'PM_HIERARCHY_CASCADE',
   291→        NEW.completed_by,
   292→        jsonb_build_object(
   293→            'source_pm', pm_code,
   294→            'satisfied_pms', satisfied_codes,
   295→            'train_id', NEW.train_id
   296→        ),
   297→        NOW()
   298→    );
   299→
   300→    RETURN NEW;
   301→END;
   302→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   303→
   304→-- Trigger for PM schedule completion
   305→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction ON pm_schedule;
   306→CREATE TRIGGER trg_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction
   307→    AFTER UPDATE ON pm_schedule
   308→    FOR EACH ROW
   309→    EXECUTE FUNCTION apply_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction();
   310→
   311→-- =============================================================================
   312→-- AUDIT TRAIL TRIGGERS
   313→-- =============================================================================
   314→
   315→-- Automatic audit logging for pm_schedule changes
   316→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION audit_pm_schedule_changes()
   317→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   318→BEGIN
   319→    INSERT INTO pm_schedule_history (
   320→        pm_schedule_id,
   321→        change_type,
   322→        old_values,
   323→        new_values,
   324→        changed_by,
   325→        change_reason,
   326→        created_at
   327→    ) VALUES (
   328→        COALESCE(NEW.id, OLD.id),
   329→        TG_OP,
   330→        CASE WHEN TG_OP IN ('UPDATE', 'DELETE') THEN to_jsonb(OLD) ELSE NULL END,
   331→        CASE WHEN TG_OP IN ('INSERT', 'UPDATE') THEN to_jsonb(NEW) ELSE NULL END,
   332→        COALESCE(NEW.updated_by, OLD.updated_by, current_setting('app.current_user_id', TRUE)::UUID),
   333→        current_setting('app.change_reason', TRUE),
   334→        NOW()
   335→    );
   336→
   337→    RETURN COALESCE(NEW, OLD);
   338→END;
   339→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   340→
   341→DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_audit_pm_schedule ON pm_schedule;
   342→CREATE TRIGGER trg_audit_pm_schedule
   343→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON pm_schedule
   344→    FOR EACH ROW
   345→    EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_pm_schedule_changes();
   346→
   347→-- =============================================================================
   348→-- VERIFICATION
   349→-- =============================================================================
   350→
   351→DO $$
   352→DECLARE
   353→    func_count INTEGER;
   354→    trigger_count INTEGER;
   355→BEGIN
   356→    -- Verify functions exist
   357→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO func_count
   358→    FROM pg_proc
   359→    WHERE proname IN (
   360→        'update_updated_at_column',
   361→        'sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability',
   362→        'refresh_canvas_views',
   363→        'cleanup_expired_scenarios',
   364→        'release_expired_reservations',
   365→        'update_fleet_mileage_targets',
   366→        'apply_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction',
   367→        'audit_pm_schedule_changes'
   368→    );
   369→
   370→    IF func_count < 8 THEN
   371→        RAISE WARNING 'Not all functions created. Expected 8, found %', func_count;
   372→    END IF;
   373→
   374→    -- Verify triggers exist
   375→    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO trigger_count
   376→    FROM pg_trigger
   377→    WHERE tgname IN (
   378→        'trg_sync_train_fitness',
   379→        'trg_pm_hierarchy_satisfaction',
   380→        'trg_audit_pm_schedule'
   381→    );
